description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the ruby:2.3 Docker image and sets the JEKYLL_ENV variable to production. Apply a before_script that runs `cd source` then `bundle install` for every job. Define two stages, test and deploy. In the test stage add a `test` job that runs `bundle exec jekyll build -d test`, stores the `source/test` directory as an artifact, and runs on all branches except master. In the deploy stage add a `pages` job that runs `bundle exec jekyll build -d public`, then `cd ..` and `mv source/public ./public`, publishes the `public` directory as an artifact, and only runs on the master branch.
